title: ranunculus acris subsp. novus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. novus, also known as the lesser spearwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health, providing benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus acris subsp. novus can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
